task: Bring project-dashboard participant creation into the GOV.UK Nunjucks render flow, prefer D1 canonical identifiers, and capture participant real names as sensitive operational data.

roleConsultation.researchOperations: Capture real first and family names because recruitment, consent, withdrawal, scheduling and safeguarding need operational identity. Keep participant reference as the default display label.
roleConsultation.userResearch: Real names are justified fieldwork operations data. Default lists must remain pseudonymised.
roleConsultation.privacy: Names are personal data. Store them behind the existing sensitive-details boundary and do not expose them in default list responses.
roleConsultation.security: Keep real names behind the existing participant.pii.reveal route and audit reveal attempts.
roleConsultation.interactionDesignAndContentDesign: Use separate GOV.UK first name and family name fields. Explain why names are collected and that participant references remain the default list label.

scopeControls: D1 remains canonical for participant create, list and reveal behaviour.
Airtable remains secondary and is not introduced as a runtime dependency.
Default participant list responses remain pseudonymised.
First name, family name and full name are returned only from the permission-gated reveal route.
No new D1 table is introduced in this branch.
residualRisks: The study participants page still contains older inline add-participant behaviour.
A later branch may split names into a dedicated private participant table if retention or field-level audit requirements increase.
